embed.alt: High-energy comedy trailer for “St. Paddy’s Beer Crawl.” Set in Chicago on St. Patrick’s Day—establishing aerial of the neon-green river, packed streets flooded with people in green. Smash cut into a rapid montage: a group of friends sprinting out of a crowded Irish pub, marking off a crumpled “ultimate crawl list.” Quick edits escalate the chaos—pints slamming on tables, one friend losing their balance mid-toast, another pulled into a loud singalong, someone spinning in a blur of green beads, door-to-door pub hopping. Handheld camera, whip pans, motion blur, bursts of slow-motion foam splashes, flashing bar lights. Dialogue layered over a driving Irish punk soundtrack: “How many stops left?” / “Too many!” / “We finish this!” Pace accelerates—sunset to night in flashes, faces getting more exhausted but determined. Final shot: the group staring at one last glowing pub entrance. Smash cut to black. Title card hits hard: “ST. PADDY’S BEER CRAWL.”

images.alt:
A dark, mythic digital painting of a faceless feminine figure centered against a smoky black and gold backdrop. Her head is replaced by an ornate oval mirror framed in baroque gold, its cracked surface glowing softly as it reflects diffuse golden light. A radiant circular halo of fine gold lines forms behind her, giving the composition a sacred, icon-like presence.
Her body is wrapped in a tight lattice of black ribbon-like bands, forming diamond-shaped openings across her skin. The lattice feels both decorative and restraining, subtly stretching across her torso and hips, hinting at underlying structure and tension beneath the surface. A faint outward contour at the hips adds quiet anatomical definition.
A large golden serpent coils behind her shoulders, partially veiled by sheer dark fabric. Smaller serpents appear subtly within the lattice—one near her wrist and another along her side—blending into the structure so that their presence is not immediately obvious.
She cradles a decaying apple at mid-torso level. The fruit is bruised and split with a vertical wound, from which molten gold pours downward in a slow, glowing stream. The liquid drifts slightly as it falls, like viscous light or warm smoke.
The apple emits a soft golden glow that illuminates her hands and the surrounding lattice, faintly reflecting into the cracked mirror above. The image blends sacred and unsettling elements, evoking themes of temptation, transformation, restraint, and the offering of something both luminous and corrupted.

images.alt:
Dark symbolic illustration of a mysterious woman standing against a black background, rendered in dramatic gold and shadow tones. Her face is replaced by an ornate oval mirror framed with filigree, the reflective surface pale and blank, giving the figure an anonymous, archetypal presence. Above her head floats a faint halo-like circle.

Multiple golden serpents coil around her shoulders and upper body, their bodies looping in elegant curves that frame the figure. She wears flowing, elaborate garments with intricate ornamental patterns that obscure most of her body beneath layered fabric.

In both hands she holds a glowing golden apple at chest height. The apple emits warm light that illuminates her hands and the surrounding cloth. A stream of molten gold pours from the fruit and runs downward through the center of the composition like liquid light.

The overall mood is mythic and enigmatic, evoking themes of temptation, knowledge, transformation, and sacred symbolism through the apple, serpent imagery, and the faceless mirror figure.
